title: lichen_lora_l2_start() races with concurrent start calls
description:
Location: lora_l2.c:261-325

**What goes wrong:** Two threads calling lichen_lora_l2_start() concurrently can both pass the running check at line 283 (before either sets the atomic), then both call lora_config() and k_thread_create(). The second k_thread_create() reuses rx_thread_data while the first RX thread is still running, corrupting thread state.

**Trigger:** Application code that calls start() from multiple threads or contexts (e.g., network stack init + application init both trying to start LoRa).

**Fix:** Move the atomic_set(running, 1) inside the mutex-protected section, or use atomic_cas to claim the running state atomically before proceeding.
